analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition
analysis.dtype: fp32 (TF32 enabled — cuDNN implicit-GEMM convs run on Tensor Cores)

time_budget._note: Budget computed over the per-call Aten operator entries (the IR nodes FX passes target). The 'layer::unique::prologue' NVTX wrapper (460671 ns) and the generic call_index=0 aggregates (aten::cudnn_convolution_0 324064 ns, aten::convolution_0 5888 ns, aten::addmm_0 4800 ns) are EXCLUDED from the budget because they re-aggregate the same kernels already attributed per-op-id, and including them double-counts (fused_kernel_double_count). pct is relative to the de-duplicated per-call total = 434303 ns.

operators: BatchNorm pointwise kernels (triton_poi/red/per_fused__native_batch_*, 14 unattributed + ~14 inside prologue)

bottleneck.description: Inference-mode BatchNorm2d is realized as a chain of standalone memory-bound Triton pointwise/reduction kernels (triton_poi_fused__native_batch_3..7, triton_red_fused__native_batch_8, triton_per_fused__native_batch_9) that are launched after every convolution. Each reads and rewrites the full activation tensor for an affine transform y = gamma*(x-mean)/sqrt(var+eps)+beta. In eval() the running mean/var are frozen, so this is mathematically a constant per-channel scale+shift that can be folded into the preceding conv's weights and bias. The conv layers were defined with bias=False, leaving a clean slot for the folded bias. These kernels carry zero Tensor-Core activity and are pure DRAM traffic, so they add latency and memory bandwidth pressure with no compute value.

transformation.description: Fold each eval-mode BatchNorm2d into the weights and bias of the immediately preceding convolution (Conv-BN folding). Compute scale = gamma / sqrt(running_var + eps); set W_folded[oc] = W[oc] * scale[oc] and b_folded[oc] = beta[oc] - running_mean[oc] * scale[oc]. After folding, the native_batch_norm node and all its associated Triton pointwise/reduction kernels are removed from the graph; the conv now emits the already-normalized output directly. This eliminates the per-conv BN kernel chain (the 14 unattributed triton_*_native_batch_* kernels plus their duplicates inside the prologue range).
transformation.location: FX Aten IR: every (aten::convolution.default | aten::cudnn_convolution.default) -> aten::_native_batch_norm_legit_no_training.default pair in stage1/stage2/stage3.
fx_steps: weight, bias, running_mean, running_var = node.args[1], node.args[2], node.args[3], node.args[4]; eps = node.args[5]
# materialize folded params as new get_attr constants on gm
# rebind conv weight arg to W_folded get_attr, inject b_folded as conv bias arg (was None / no-bias)
node.replace_all_uses_with(bn_input)  # BN consumers now read folded-conv output
transformation.code_hint: Prefer the maintained helper torch.nn.utils.fuse_conv_bn_weights(conv_w, conv_b, bn_rm, bn_rv, bn_eps, bn_w, bn_b) to compute folded tensors; or apply torch.fx.experimental.optimization.fuse(model) on the eval module before torch.compile. Verify the conv node is the SOLE consumer of its output before folding (BN identity assumes one downstream BN).

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Removes one full-activation read+write per BN per conv (3 stages x 2 iters). Largest stage1 activation is 16M-element NHWC; eliminating its 16384-block BN sweep is the biggest single saving.

optimizations.notes: Numerically exact in eval mode (folding is an identity). Must run with model in .eval() and frozen running stats. If torch.compile/inductor already folds some BN, the residual standalone triton_*_native_batch_* kernels in this profile prove folding is incomplete pre-Inductor — doing it at FX level before backend lowering guarantees removal.

operators: convertTensor_kernel layout-conversion launches (~20 across the graph)

bottleneck.description: The model and input are in NCHW (channels-first) but cuDNN routes the heavy convolutions to its fast NHWC implicit-GEMM Tensor-Core path ('Kernel' / sm80_xmma_fprop_implicit_gemm). cuDNN therefore inserts convertTensor_kernel NCHW<->NHWC reformatting kernels before and after each conv. These conversions are pure DRAM streaming with zero Tensor-Core activity, and several run at 55-73% of peak DRAM throughput — i.e. they saturate memory bandwidth doing nothing but shuffling layout. They surround every conv (2-3 per conv) and are repeated each iteration.

transformation.description: Convert the whole module and the input tensor to torch.channels_last (NHWC) so data stays in cuDNN's preferred layout end-to-end. Once every conv input/output is already NHWC, cuDNN no longer needs the convertTensor reformatting round-trips, and the high-bandwidth layout-shuffle kernels collapse to (near) zero. The compute 'Kernel' convs already run at 58-65% Tensor-Core utilization in NHWC, so this is purely about feeding them without re-layout overhead.
transformation.location: Model construction / input pipeline; expressed in Aten IR as a memory_format propagation. The conv nodes (aten::cudnn_convolution.default) keep identical args; only the stride/memory-format metadata of their input/weight tensors changes.
fx_steps: # Optional FX assertion pass: confirm no aten convert/clone-to-NCHW reappears between conv nodes
assert n.args[0].meta.get('tensor_meta') is None or n.args[0].meta['tensor_meta'].memory_format == torch.channels_last
transformation.code_hint: channels_last is set on the nn.Module + input, not via node surgery; the FX pass only verifies the layout propagated and that no aten::clone / aten::contiguous(memory_format=contiguous) was reinserted. Combine with OPT-1 so the folded conv weights are also stored channels_last.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Eliminates the bandwidth-saturating convertTensor NCHW<->NHWC round-trips (several at 55-73% peak DRAM). This is the largest memory-traffic source in the profile after BN.

optimizations.notes: Apply after OPT-1 so folded weights are allocated channels_last (a weight buffer laid out NCHW would re-trigger a one-time convert). Numerically identical. duration_ns figures are 2-5x inflated by ncu overhead — used only for relative ranking, not absolute latency.

bottleneck.description: After Conv-BN folding (OPT-1), each conv is followed only by a ReLU. ReLU is an elementwise pass over the full activation; if it lands in its own kernel it costs another full-tensor read+write of memory-bound traffic with no compute. The profile shows ReLU/activation work already partly folded into the BN triton kernels (triton_poi_fused__native_batch_* names imply fusion), but once BN is folded into the conv weights the surviving ReLU must be re-attached to the conv epilogue or to a single pointwise kernel rather than spawning a standalone launch.

evidence.kernel: triton_poi_fused__native_batch_5 (BN+act fused pointwise, proxy for the elementwise epilogue cost)

transformation.description: Fuse the ReLU into the convolution epilogue (cuDNN supports a fused conv+bias+ReLU activation) or, under Inductor, let the relu be scheduled into the conv's epilogue/pointwise group so it does not require an independent full-tensor pass. At Aten IR this is matching aten::relu.default whose sole input is a folded aten::cudnn_convolution.default output and marking it for epilogue fusion / rewriting to aten::clamp_min.default(x, 0) that Inductor can fuse into the adjacent kernel.
transformation.location: FX Aten IR: aten::relu.default nodes immediately consuming a (folded) aten::cudnn_convolution.default output, in each ConvBnRelu block.
fx_steps: # tag for epilogue fusion; under Inductor, rewrite to clamp_min so it fuses into the conv-output pointwise group
transformation.code_hint: Inductor will typically already fuse a trailing relu/clamp_min into the conv-output pointwise kernel; the value of this pass is guaranteeing the relu does not survive as a standalone launch after BN folding changes the graph shape. Keep confidence medium since the win overlaps Inductor's default scheduler behavior.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Avoids one extra full-activation read+write per conv stage if ReLU would otherwise spill into its own kernel post-folding.

optimizations.notes: Depends on OPT-1 (BN must be folded first so the conv->relu adjacency exists). Capped at medium confidence: knowledge/fx-patterns.md was unavailable in this repo, and Inductor's default epilogue fusion likely already captures much of this benefit.

bottleneck.description: The classifier GEMM (256->10, batch 16) runs on the SIMT path (gemmSN_TN_kernel) with Tensor Cores idle: M=16, N=10 is far below the tile size needed to engage Tensor Cores, so cuBLAS picks a small SIMT kernel. The launch grid is (2,2,1) = 4 blocks against ~188 SMs, leaving the device almost entirely idle (achieved_occupancy 8.1%, sm_throughput 0.23%, eligible_cycles_pct 6.8% -> severely latency-bound under the Blackwell <20% rule). The op is tiny in absolute terms.

transformation.description: No graph transformation meaningfully helps a 16x256x10 GEMM: it is intrinsically too small to fill the GPU or to reach Tensor-Core tile thresholds, and it accounts for under 2% of attributed time. The actionable note is that it cannot be improved by fusion or layout; the only structural lever (raising batch size, or fusing the flatten+addmm so it does not gate on a tiny separate launch) yields negligible absolute savings. Recommend leaving as-is.
transformation.location: FX Aten IR: aten::addmm.default classifier node consuming the flattened pooled features.
fx_steps: # A fused flatten+addmm or a fixed batch >= 128 would let cuBLAS pick a larger tile,
# but the absolute saving is < a few microseconds of real (de-inflated) time.
transformation.code_hint: Skip — included only so the budget is fully accounted for. Do not spend effort here.

optimizations.notes: Bottleneck identified (wave starvation + sub-tile GEMM on SIMT path) but correctly judged not worth a graph transform. Listed to close out the time budget.

global_notes: knowledge/hardware-limits.md and knowledge/fx-patterns.md were NOT present in this repository checkout. Per the documented fallback, analysis proceeded using built-in architecture knowledge; novel/uncertain transforms (OPT-3) are capped at medium confidence.

SM count for the RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition was assumed ~188 SMs (GB202 full-die class) because the hardware-limits.md lookup table was unavailable. This affects only the Waves/SM commentary on OPT-4, not the ranked transforms.

Blackwell architecture: warp_cycles_per_instruction is null throughout the profile (removed on Blackwell). Latency-bound determination used eligible_cycles_pct < 20% (e.g. the addmm classifier at 6.8%).
duration_ns values are 2-5x inflated by ncu counter-collection overhead and are used ONLY for relative ranking within this profile, never as absolute latency.

Edge case fused_kernel_double_count: the per-op-id aten::cudnn_convolution entries have is_fused=true and re-aggregate kernels also counted in the layer::unique::prologue NVTX wrapper and in the generic call_index=0 aggregates. The time budget was de-duplicated by reasoning over the per-call op-id entries only and excluding prologue + generic aggregates; pct is normalized to 434303 ns.

Edge case warm_up_inflation: kernel_count values (3 per per-call op, 15 and 41 for the generic/prologue aggregates) are not clean multiples of the apparent measure_iters=2 (op_ids repeat as the sequence 7/12/18/21 then 28/33/39/42). Per-call entries appear to hold one invocation's kernels while the aggregates span both iterations; treat absolute kernel_counts as approximate.

Dominant compute (stage2 op12/op33 ~48% and stage3 op18/op39 ~42% of attributed time) is already well-optimized: the 'Kernel' implicit-GEMM convs run at 58-65% Tensor-Core utilization and 58-64% sm_throughput. They are compute-bound and need no graph rewrite themselves; the wins are in the memory-bound material AROUND them (OPT-1 BN folding, OPT-2 channels-last) which also lets those convs run without re-layout stalls.

Dependency DAG: OPT-1 (Conv-BN fold) -> OPT-2 (channels_last, so folded weights are allocated NHWC) -> OPT-3 (ReLU epilogue fusion, requires the conv->relu adjacency created by OPT-1). OPT-4 is independent and is a no-op recommendation. No cycles.

Operators below 1% threshold omitted from the budget table: 0 standalone sub-1% Aten ops in the de-duplicated set (the classifier addmm at 1.8% is retained; the generic aggregates and prologue were excluded as double-counts, not as sub-threshold).
